channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: After Columbia arrests, international college students fall silent
item.description: International students and foreign faculty have watched the crackdown on pro-Palestinian protesters at Columbia University with apprehension.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Gecker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrats confront limits of their minority power after bruising shutdown vote
item.description: Senate Democrats were grim Friday as they left Washington after a brutal 10-week stretch that consistently showed the limits of their new minority and culminated with a deeply personal rupture over how to best counter President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House lawmakers hold hearing to further investigate Florida insurance companies
item.description: House lawmakers gathered at the statehouse Friday for a hearing to look into a bombshell report that Florida insurance companies were "ripping off" Floridians and misleading the legislature, claiming to be broke.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he was being a 'bit sarcastic' when he promised to end Russia-Ukraine war in 24 hours
item.description: President Donald Trump says he was “being a little bit sarcastic” when he repeatedly claimed as a candidate that he would have the Russia-Ukraine war solved within 24 hours — and before he even took office.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Appeals court lifts blocks on Trump's orders restricting diversity, equity and inclusion programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An appeals court on Friday lifted a block on executive orders seeking to end government support for diversity, equity and inclusion programs, handing the Trump administration a win after a string of setbacks defending President Donald Trump’s agenda from dozens of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma schools chief won’t abandon classroom Bible effort
item.description: Last year, Ryan Walters issued a mandate for schools to incorporate lessons on the Bible in grades 5 through 12.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Senate unanimously approves fix to prevent $1B cuts for District of Columbia, sending bill to House
item.description: The Senate has swiftly approved legislation that would allow the District of Columbia to keep its budget intact.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FBI investigating rise in swatting incidents after several conservatives targeted, Kash Patel says
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel on Friday said that the agency is investigating the recent surge in swatting incidents on conservative media figures and others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man escorted out of NC Republican's town hall
item.description: Rep. Chuck Edwards (R-N.C.) faced a wave of backlash from constituents while hosting a Thursday town hall in Asheville, which he held against the advice of party leaders. One attendee, who identified himself as a veteran, was removed from the event. “He has nothing to say but lies.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Canadian reporters grill Rubio over Trump's 51st state talk
item.description: Rubio said Trump’s stance on Canada joining the U.S. came following a dinner conversation with former Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Migrant gangs put on notice after string of home burglaries
item.description: Lawmakers push the Department of Homeland Security to drop Chile from the visa waiver program, warning that gangs are exploiting it for organized burglaries in U.S. communities and businesses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden White House turned over Trump, Pence government cellphones to FBI as part of anti-Trump agent's case
item.description: The Biden White House turned over government cellphones belonging to President Donald Trump and former Vice President Mike Pence to the FBI in May 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump takes birthright citizenship to the Supreme Court
item.description: The president's contention that birthright citizenship is unconstitutional is considered a fringe view because the Supreme Court ruled to the contrary 127 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Hochul contacted NYC Council speaker before she announced mayoral run
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Among the calls New York City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams took before hopping into the race for mayor was from Gov. Kathy Hochul. "I heard from the governor," said Adrienne Adams.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Democrats set to host town halls targeting vulnerable House Republicans
item.description: Democratic committees are set to host town halls across the country to target vulnerable House Republicans ahead of the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rep. Raúl Grijalva dies at 77
item.description: Rep. Raúl Grijalva (D-Ariz.), a more than two-decade veteran of the House who rose to high levels of power in the Democratic Caucus, died on Thursday. He was 77.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security says it will no longer accept bank changes over the phone
item.description: Those who need to change their direct deposit information with Social Security will have to do it online or in-person.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of fired federal workers must be offered reinstatement, a judge rules
item.description: Thousands of probationary federal employees fired by the Trump administration must be offered job reinstatement, a judge in San Francisco has ruled, because they were terminated unlawfully.
item.dc:creator: Chris Arnold and Emily Feng, MPR News

item.title: Federal judge rules Trump must reinstate many fired federal employees
item.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday that the Trump administration must reinstate probationary government employees fired unlawfully at several agencies, lambasting the Justice Department at a hearing for a “sham” gambit that enabled a key official to avoid testifying in the case. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: White House withdraws nomination of David Weldon to run the CDC
item.description: Dr. Dave Weldon, Trump's pick for director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, was withdrawn from consideration shortly before a scheduled Senate confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Activist Mahmoud Khalil's arrest 'deeply concerning': Ex-NYC mayor
item.description: "You don't get arrested for disagreeing with the U.S. government," said former NYC Mayor Bill de Blasio.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Tariffs stoke anxiety with Minnesota officials over uncertain impacts
item.description: Minnesota U.S. Senator Amy Klobuchar said she’s concerned about the consequences the tariffs imposed by the Trump administration. She spoke at a press conference Wednesday that featured a Minnesota farmer who worries about the impacts on agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Nevada cuts wait time for inmates needing mental health care
item.description: The wait time for inmates needing a mental health bed to regain competency was down to an average 74 days compared to 78 when the 8 News Now Investigators reported on the statewide problem last fall.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Walz warns Department of Education cuts could put students, schools at risk
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz said cuts at the Department of Education could cause budget troubles and confusion at public schools across the state, after the department said it’s cutting half its staff.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump vows to take back 'stolen' wealth as tariffs on steel and aluminum imports go into effect
item.description: President Donald Trump is openly challenging U.S. allies by increasing tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports to 25 percent as he vows to take back wealth he says was “stolen” by other countries, drawing quick retaliation from Europe and Canada.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 56 percent disapprove of Trump handling of economy: Survey
item.description: The survey comes as Trump faces criticism for his tariff policies and as the stock market has seen significant dips amid concerns of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: The U.S. agency that monitors weather will cut another 1,000 jobs, AP sources say
item.description: Multiple sources tell The Associated Press that the Trump Administration is starting another round of job cuts — this one more than 1,000 — at the nation’s weather, ocean and fisheries agency.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: EPA signals it could narrow Clean Water Act protections
item.description: The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) has signaled that it could narrow which set of waters receive protections under the Clean Water Act.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Mark Carney, elected next Canadian prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney, a former central banker, won his Liberal Party's election in a landslide on Sunday to replace Justin Trudeau as prime minister of Canada. He will be sworn into office in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams’ approval rating reaches record low ahead of election
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – The mayor’s race is heating up just as Mayor Eric Adams’ approval rating hits a new low, according to a new poll from Quinnipiac University. A new poll shows only 20% of voters approve of how Adams is handling his post, while 67% disapprove.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11
